question:
Which of the following statements are correct?
(I) Some species of Asteraceae and grasses have evolved a special mechanism to produce seeds without fertilisation.
(II) In Citrus plants, the ovule contains many embryos which develop from nucellar cells.
(III) In mango, the ovule contains one embryo which develops from nucellar cells.
(IV) The occurrence of more than one embryo in a seed is referred to as apomixis.
explanation: Two are correct (I and II )As in many Citrus and Mango varieties some of the nucellar cells surrounding the embryo sac start dividing, protrude into the embryo sac and develop into the embryos. In such species each ovule contains many embryos. Occurrence of more than one embryo in a seed is referred to as polyembryony.

It would drastically reduce the cost of hybrid production, so that plant breeders produce new varieties of seeds more quickly and more cheaply.

question: Identify the correct order of steps involved in artificial hybridization in plants.
explanation:
Artificial hybridization of plant involves following steps:
• Anthers or stamens are removed, or pollen grains are killed in a bisexual flower of a plant to be used as the female parent in a cross, a process called emasculation.
• Emasculated flower is immediately bagged to avoid pollination by any foreign pollen grains. These bags are made up of butter paper, fine cloth or polythene depending on the inflorescence. Bagged flowers are then tagged.
• Mature and fertile pollen grains are collected from male parent, bag is opened, and pollen grains are dusted on receptive stigma with the help of brush and artificial pollination is done.
• Then, bagging is repeated.
subject: Biology

question: Endosperm is generally
explanation: Endosperms is produced inside the seeds of most flowering plants around the time of fertilization. It is formed when the two sperm nuclei inside a pollen grain reach the interior of an embryo sac or female gametophyte. One sperm nucleus fertilizes the egg,forming a zygote, while the other sperm nucleus usually fuses with the two polar nuclei at the centre of the embryo sac, forming a primary endosperm cell (its nucleus is often called the triple fusion nucleus). About $70 \%$ of angiosperm species have endosperm cells that are polyploid. These are typically triploid (containing three sets of chromosomes) but can vary widely from diploid (2n) to $15 n$.
subject: Biology

question:
Read the following statements carefully and identify the correct statements:
(I) Annual plants are always monocarpic.
(II) All seeded plants are heterosporous.
(III) The sporophyte is completely independent in angiosperms.
(IV) The male gametophyte of angiosperms is the most reduced and has three cells at maturity.
explanation: All are correct. Annual plants are always monocarpic. All seeded plants are heterosporous. Sporophyte is completely independent in angiosperm. Male gametophyte of angiosperm is most reduced and have three cells at maturity.
subject: Biology

question: How many reduction divisions are required to produce 120 antipodal cells in a Polygonum-type embryo sac?
explanation: 40 Meiosis (reduction division) will result in 120 antipodal cells, as one embryo sac has 3 antipodals.
subject: Biology

question: What will happen in the functional megaspore of an angiosperm?
explanation: The mitotic divisions in angiosperms are strictly free nuclear, that is, nuclear divisions are not followed immediately by cell wall formation. After the 8 - nucleate stage, cell walls are laid down leading to the organisation of the typical female gametophyte.

options.text: The embryo sac is formed by mitosis, then meiosis.
The embryo sac is formed by three mitotic nuclear divisions, with cell walls laid down later.
The embryo sac is formed by three mitotic nuclear divisions, with nuclear divisions followed immediately by cell wall formation.
The embryo sac is formed by two mitotic nuclear divisions, with cell walls laid down later.

subject: Biology

question:
Which of the following statements is true regarding polar nuclei?
(I) Polar nuclei are haploid.
(II) They fuse to form the secondary nucleus just after fertilization.
(III) Polar nuclei are situated below the egg apparatus.
(IV) They are present in the large central cell.
explanation: I, III, IV are correct The polar nuclei on fertilisation becomes the primary endosperm nucleus.

question:
Which of the following statements is true for the nucellus?
(I) It provides nutrition to the developing embryo sac.
(II) It is present inside the embryo sac.
(III) It is diploid tissue.
(IV) It is enclosed within the integuments.
(V) Ovules generally differentiate a single megaspore mother cell (MMC) in the micropylar region of the nucellus.
(VI) The embryo sac is present inside it.
explanation: I, III, IV, V, VI are correct. Enclosed within the integuments is a mass of cells called the nucellus. Cells of the nucellus have abundant reserve food materials. Located in the nucellus is the embryo sac or female gametophyte.

options.text: I, II, IV, V are correct
I, III, IV, V, VI are correct

subject: Biology

question: The ploidy level of the nucellus, integument, and antipodal cells of a plant, respectively, is:

question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct about ovules in an ovary?
(I) The number of ovules in an ovary may be one to many.
(II) The number of ovules in an ovary may be one (e.g. orchids, paddy, mango).
(III) The number of ovules in an ovary may be many (e.g. papaya, watermelon, wheat).
explanation: Only I is correct. The number of ovules in an ovary may be one (wheat, paddy, mango) to many (papaya, water melon, orchids).
subject: Biology

question: Which of the following plants have many ovules in their ovaries?
explanation: The number of ovules in an ovary may be one (wheat, paddy, mango) to many (papaya, water melon, orchids).

Which of the following statements about sporopollenin is incorrect?
(I) Exine is made up mainly of sporopollenin.
(II) Sporopollenin is one of the most resistant organic materials.

question:
Select the correct statement(s) regarding the structure of an embryo sac:
(I) The egg apparatus consists of two synergids and two egg cell.
(II) The large central cell has three polar nuclei.
(III) The synergids have special cellular thickenings at the chalazal tip called the filiform apparatus.
(IV) Polar nuclei are situated below the egg apparatus in the large central cell.
explanation: The egg apparatus, consists of two synergids and one egg cell. The synergids have special cellular thickenings at the micropylar tip called filiform apparatus, which play an important role in guiding the pollen tubes into the synergid. The large central cell, has two polar nuclei.
subject: Biology

question:
Select the correct statement(s) regarding the structure of a mature embryo sac:
(I) The egg apparatus is situated towards the chalazal end.
(II) Antipodal cells are situated towards the micropylar end.
(III) A typical angiospermous embryo sac is an 8-celled, 7-nucleate structure.
(IV) A typical angiospermous embryo sac is a 7-celled, 8-nucleate structure.
explanation:
A typical angiospermous embryo sac is 7 - celled, 8 - nucleate structure is the correct statement. The egg apparatus is located towards the micropylar end.
Antipodal cells are situated at the chalazal end.
subject: Biology

question:
Cryopreservation is:
(I) The storage of pollen grains of only exotic species.
(II) The fossilization of pollen grains.
(III) The storage of pollen grains in liquid nitrogen ( $\mathrm{N}_{2}$ ) for various artificial plant breeding programs.
explanation: Only (III) is correct. Cryopreservation is storage of pollen grains in liquid $\mathrm{N}_{2}$ for various artificial plant breeding.
subject: Biology

question:

(I) It degenerates at maturity.
(II) It is polyploid.
(III) It nourishes developing pollen grains.

explanation: I, IV only are correct. The outer three wall layers perform the function of protection and help in dehiscence of anther to release the pollen. Tapetum nourishes the developing pollen grains and is polyploid.

question:
Which of the following options is correct?
(I) Parthenium, or carrot grass, which came into India as a contaminant with imported wheat, causes pollen allergy.
(II) In about $40 \%$ of angiosperms, pollination occurs at the 2-celled stage.
(III) Generative cells divide meiotically to form two male gametes.
(IV) In about $60 \%$ of angiosperms, pollination occurs when a pollen grain forms at least a vegetative and generative cell.
explanation: I and IV are correct Parthenium or carrot grass that came into India as a contaminant with imported wheat, causes pollen allergy In about 60% of angiosperm, pollination occur when a pollen grain at least form a vegetation and generative cell.

question:

(I) Stamen is a unit of Androecium.
(II) Stamen is also known as microsporophyll.
(III) Stamen is differentiated into filament and anther.
(IV) Stamen are the male sex organ of the flower.

question: How many male gametes are produced in a pea flower which have 50 microspore mother cell in one microsporangia?
explanation:
Pea is diadelphous and has 10 anthers.
1 anther has 4 microsporangia. therefore $10 \times 4=40$ microsporangia.
1 PMC produces 4 microspores/pollen grain $50 \mathrm{~PMC}=200$ pollen grain;
One pollen grain has 2 male gametes. $40 \times 200 \times 2=16,000$.

question:
Given below are two statements : One is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion: A typical unfertilised, angiosperm embryo sac at maturity is 8 nucleate and 7-celled.

Reason: The egg apparatus has 2 polar nuclei.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
explanation:
The correct answer is option 4.
Assertion (A): True – A mature angiosperm embryo sac is 8-nucleate and 7-celled (3 cells in egg apparatus, 2 synergids, 1 egg cell, 2 polar nuclei in central cell, 3 antipodals).
Reason (R): False – The egg apparatus has 1 egg cell and 2 synergids, not polar nuclei. The 2 polar nuclei lie in the central cell, not in the egg apparatus.

options.text: Assertion is false but Reason is true
Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false

subject: Biology

question:

Reason: Presence of more than one nucleus in the tapetum increases the efficiency of nourishing the developing microspore mother cells.
In light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
explanation:
The correct answer is option 4.
Tapetal cells are nutritive cells of the anther and possess dense cytoplasm.They are often binucleate or multinucleate, which enhances their metabolic activity and supports the development of pollen grains.

options.text: Assertion is false but Reason is true
Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true but is not the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false

question: How many meiotic and mitotic divisions need to occur for the development of a mature female gametophyte from the megaspore mother cell in an angiosperm plant?
explanation:
The correct answer is option 4.
In angiosperms, the megaspore mother cell undergoes 1 meiotic division to form 4 haploid megaspores, of which 1 survives. This functional megaspore then undergoes 3 mitotic divisions to form the
8-nucleate embryo sac (female gametophyte).Total divisions: 1 meiosis + 3 mitosis

question: Microsporogenesis occurs
explanation: Microsporogenesis is the process of formation of microspores or pollen grains, from a pollen mother cell through meiosis is micro sporogenesis Each cell of sporogenous tissue serve as microspore mother cell (MMC). These MMCs undergo meiosis and form microspore tetrad and become haploid, microspores or pollen grains. As anthers mature microspores of the tetrad separate from each other and develops into pollen grains. Each microsporangium contains numerous pollen grains which are released after dehiscence of anther wall.

question: If there are 4 cells (PMC) in anthers, what will be the number of pollen grains?
explanation: Microspores or pollen grains develop inside the anther, that is the fertile part of stamen or microsporophyll. The primary sporogenous cell produces microspore mother cells or pollen mother cells (MMC or PMC), inside the anther. On reduction division, every MMC produces 4 pollens or microspores. Accordingly, these four cells will produce 4 x 4 = 16 pollen grains.
subject: Biology

question: Pollen grains develop from
explanation: The microsporangia develops to form pollen sacs. When the anther is young, sporogenous tissue (a group of compactly arranged homogenous cells) captures the central position of each microsporangium. Cells of the sporogenous tissue further gives rise to microspore tetrad.
subject: Biology

question: Male gametophyte of angiosperm is
explanation: Microspore is haploid, uninucleate, minute spore produced in large numbers as a result of meiosis in microspore mother cells inside the microsporangia. These are the first cell of gametophytic generation in angiosperm.
subject: Biology

question: Number of meiotic divisions required to produce 200/400 seeds of pea would be
explanation: Number of meiotic divisions required to produce 200/400 seeds of pea would be 250/500. 200 seeds of pea would be produced from 200 pollen grains and 200 eggs. 200 pollen grains will be formed by 50 microspore mother cell while 200 eggs will be formed by 200 megaspore mother cell so 250/500.
subject: Biology

question: Male gametes in the angiosperms are formed by the division of
explanation: The male gametes of angiosperms consist of two sperm cells within a pollen grain or a pollen tube. They are derived from a single generative cell, which is formed as the smaller cell by unequal cell division in the microspore after meiosis.
subject: Biology

question:
Arrange the following wall layer of anther from inner side to the outside
i. Tapetum   ii. Endothecium
iii. Epidermis   iv. Middle layer
explanation:
The microsporangium is generally surrounded by four wall layers from outside to inside – the epidermis, endothecium, middle layers and the tapetum. Therefore the correct
order is i, iv, ii, iii.
subject: Biology

question: Tapetal cells of stamens are
explanation: Cells of the tapetum possess dense cytoplasm and generally have more than one nucleus. The tapetum is a layer of nutritive cells found within the sporangium, particularly within the anther of flowering plants. The cells are usually bigger and normally have more than one nucleus per cell. As the sporogenous cells undergo mitosis, the nuclei of tapetal cells also divide. Sometimes, this mitosis is not normal due to which many cells of mature tapetum become multinucleate.
subject: Biology

explanation: The outermost and innermost wall layers of microsporangium in an anther are Epidermis and tapetum respectively.

question: In the anther wall
explanation: Periclinally the archesporial cells divide forming outer sub - epidermal parietal cells and inner sporogenous cells. Thus, the microsporangial wall consists of four kind of epidermal layer, endothecium, 1-3 middle layers and tapetum.

play a predominant role in its dehiscence
explanation: The hypodermal layer of the anther wall present just beneath the epidermis is called as endothecium and remains in the mature anther.
